flashcards.front: What is the primary difference between R-CNN and YOLO in object detection?
flashcards.back: R-CNN uses region proposals and multiple stages, while YOLO performs detection in a single pass through the network for real-time processing.

flashcards.front: What does IoU (Intersection over Union) measure in object detection?
flashcards.back: IoU measures overlap between predicted bounding box and ground truth, calculated as area of intersection divided by area of union.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) in YOLO?
flashcards.back: NMS eliminates redundant bounding boxes by keeping only the ones with highest confidence scores while suppressing overlapping predictions.

flashcards.front: What are anchor boxes in YOLO?
flashcards.back: Predefined template boxes of different aspect ratios that help predict multiple objects in the same grid cell.

flashcards.front: What are the three main components of Faster R-CNN?
flashcards.back: 1. Region Proposal Network (RPN) 2. ROI Pooling 3. Classification and bounding box regression heads

flashcards.front: Why does YOLO divide the input image into a grid?
flashcards.back: Each grid cell predicts bounding boxes and class probabilities, enabling efficient object localization across the entire image.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of YOLO over R-CNN variants?
flashcards.back: YOLO's single-stage architecture enables real-time detection while maintaining reasonable accuracy.

flashcards.front: What problem does ROI Pooling solve in R-CNN?
flashcards.back: It converts variable-sized region proposals into fixed-size feature maps for consistent classifier input.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of hard negative mining in R-CNN?
flashcards.back: Improves classifier performance by selectively training on difficult false-positive examples.

flashcards.front: What does 'You Only Look Once' refer to in YOLO?
flashcards.back: The algorithm processes the image through the CNN only once to make all predictions simultaneously.

flashcards.front: What is the main limitation of original R-CNN?
flashcards.back: Computational inefficiency due to processing ~2000 region proposals per image through CNN separately.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the Region Proposal Network (RPN) in Faster R-CNN?
flashcards.back: RPN generates region proposals directly from CNN feature maps using anchor boxes and objectness scores.
